words.clue: Symptom of a troubled world preceding the Second Advent, mentioned in the Olivet discourse.
words.expertClue: Olivet discourse symptom: nations rise against nations before the end.
words.scripture: And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
words.summary: As the end approaches, international conflicts and political instability serve as reminders that this world is temporary and the return of Christ is near.

words.clue: Deceivers who will arise to show great signs and wonders, attempting to mislead even the elect.
words.expertClue: Matthew 24 warns these deceivers may show wonders to mislead the elect.
words.scripture: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall shew great signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall deceive the very elect.
words.summary: Spiritual deception will reach its climax before the end. The only safeguard against the delusions of the enemy is a deep, personal knowledge of God's Word.

words.clue: The trial God's people will face for keeping His commandments and holding the testimony of Jesus.
words.expertClue: Matthew 13:21 — tribulation or persecution because of the word.
words.scripture: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.
words.summary: Opposition from the world reveals the true character of our faith and purifies the church, preparing believers to stand in the final conflict.

words.clue: The ultimate gospel task that must be finished in all the world as a witness before the end.
words.expertClue: Must reach every nation as a witness — then the end arrives.
words.scripture: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come.
words.summary: The end will not come until everyone has had a fair opportunity to hear and accept the message of salvation. We are called to cooperate in this great work.

words.clue: Jesus lists international conflict among signs of the end in the Olivet discourse.
words.expertClue: Matthew 24:7 — nation shall rise against nation
words.scripture: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places. 8 All these are the beginning of sorrows. 9 Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and sha
words.summary: Global unrest is a solemn reminder that history moves toward the promised return of Christ.

words.clue: A testing time among God's people where the superficial are separated from the faithful.
words.expertClue: Hebrews 12:27 — only what cannot be shaken will remain.
words.scripture: And this word, Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.
words.summary: A great sifting occurs as trials and false doctrines test the church. Only those anchored in truth and genuine experience will stand.

words.clue: The direct counsel to the Laodiceans that rouses the church and causes the shaking.
words.expertClue: Laodicean rebuke: 'As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten.'
words.scripture: As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten: be zealous therefore, and repent.
words.summary: The True Witness's testimony is a call to repentance and spiritual revival, dividing those who accept it from those who reject it in self-righteousness.

words.clue: The choice to compromise or turn away from Bible doctrines, leading to spiritual darkness during sifting.
words.expertClue: They received not the love of the truth — 2 Thessalonians 2:10.
words.scripture: And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth, that they might be saved.
words.summary: Compromise with the world and neglected truth leaves the soul vulnerable to the strong delusions of the last days.

words.clue: The purification process where the chaff of worldly members is blown away from the wheat of God's church.
words.expertClue: Amos 9:9 — sifted like corn; not one grain falls to the earth.
words.scripture: For, lo, I will command, and I will sift the house of Israel among all nations, like as corn is sifted in a sieve, yet shall not the least grain fall upon the earth.
words.summary: God permits trials to cleanse His church so that she may be presented pure, united, and ready to receive the power of the Latter Rain.

words.clue: What remains after God removes what can be shaken.
words.expertClue: Hebrews 12:27 — things which cannot be shaken may remain
words.scripture: Things which cannot be shaken may remain. 28 Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear: 29 For our God is a consuming fire. 2654 Hebrews 12
words.summary: Only truth rooted in God endures the final shaking of church and world.

words.clue: The final outpouring of the Holy Spirit to ripen the harvest and empower the loud cry.
words.expertClue: Joel 2:23 — former rain moderately, latter rain at harvest end.
words.scripture: Be glad then, ye children of Zion, and rejoice in the LORD your God: for he hath given you the former rain moderately, and he will cause to come down for you the rain, the former rain, and the latter rain in the first month.
words.summary: Just as the early rain started the church at Pentecost, the latter rain will finish God's work on earth with spectacular spiritual power.

words.clue: The historical outpouring of the Holy Spirit that empowered the early Christian church.
words.expertClue: Acts 2:1 — when the day of Pentecost was fully come.
words.scripture: And when the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place. And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.
words.summary: Pentecost was the 'early rain' of the Spirit. The latter rain will be even more abundant, covering the whole earth with the glory of God.

words.clue: Times of spiritual reviving and preparation that come from the presence of the Lord.
words.expertClue: Acts 3:19 — sins blotted out when refreshing comes from His presence.
words.scripture: Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the Lord.
words.summary: Before we can receive the fullness of the Holy Spirit, we must experience personal revival, repentance, and the blotting out of sins.

words.clue: The Divine Agent of the latter rain outpouring, promised by Christ to guide into all truth.
words.expertClue: Ephesians 1:13 — sealed with that holy Spirit of promise.
words.scripture: In whom ye also trusted, after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise,
words.summary: We must actively pray for the Holy Spirit daily, preparing our vessels to be filled with the final shower of divine grace.

words.word: YOUR SONS AND YOUR DAUGHTERS SHALL PROPHESY
words.clue: Joel’s promise of Spirit on all flesh before the great day.
words.expertClue: Joel 2:28 — your sons and your daughters shall prophesy
words.scripture: sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions: 29 And also upon the servants and upon the handmaids in those days will I pour out my spirit. 30 And I will shew wonders in the he
words.summary: The latter rain empowers the final gospel witness as Pentecost empowered the first.

words.clue: The final worldwide warning message that swells from the third angel's message.
words.expertClue: Revelation 18:1 — the earth lightened with His glory.
words.scripture: And after these things I saw another angel come down from heaven, having great power; and the earth was lightened with his glory.
words.summary: An urgent, powerful call that illuminates the entire earth, exposing deception and presenting God's final invitation of mercy to all mankind.

words.clue: The special three-fold message of Revelation 14 containing God's final warning of judgment and worship.
words.expertClue: Revelation 14's threefold final warning of judgment and worship.
words.scripture: And I saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven, having the everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on the earth... Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come...
words.summary: This message calls humanity back to worshipping the Creator, warns against false worship, and identifies the faithful who keep God's commandments.

words.clue: The second angel's declaration that the corrupt world religious systems have departed from truth.
words.expertClue: Second angel of Revelation 14 — great city fallen twice over.
words.scripture: And there followed another angel, saying, Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great city, because she made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication.
words.summary: Babylon represents confused, compromised religious bodies that have united with the state. God warns that her spiritual fall is complete.

words.clue: The urgent call of the fourth angel to leave Babylon in order to avoid her plagues.
words.expertClue: Revelation 18:4 — leave before you share in her sins and plagues.
words.scripture: And I heard another voice from heaven, saying, Come out of her, my people, that ye be not partakers of her sins, and that ye receive not of her plagues.
words.summary: God still has sincere followers within compromised religious systems. This is His loving, final appeal for them to walk in full Biblical truth.

words.word: EARTH WAS LIGHTENED WITH HIS GLORY
words.clue: Revelation 18 angel fills the world with radiant heavenly light.
words.expertClue: Revelation 18:1 — the earth was lightened with his glory
words.scripture: earth was lightened with his glory. 2 And he cried mightily with a strong voice, saying, Babylon the great is fallen, is fallen, and is become the habitation of devils, and the hold of every foul spirit, and a cage of every unclean and h
words.summary: The loud cry is not mere volume but global revelation of God’s character and warning.

words.clue: God's sign of authority, ownership, and protection placed upon the foreheads of the faithful.
words.expertClue: Revelation 7:3 — sealed in the forehead before harm to earth.
words.scripture: Saying, Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads.
words.summary: The seal of God is a settling into the truth, both intellectually and spiritually, so that believers cannot be moved.

words.clue: The seventh-day memorial of creation which serves as the core test of allegiance and the sign of the Seal.
words.expertClue: Ezekiel 20:12 — sign between God and His people as Creator.
words.scripture: Moreover also I gave them my sabbaths, to be a sign between me and them, that they might know that I am the LORD that sanctify them.
words.summary: As the commandment identifying God as Creator and Sovereign, the Sabbath is the ultimate test of loyalty in the final crisis.

words.clue: The legislative decree that enforces false worship, establishing the Mark of the Beast in contrast to God's Sabbath.
words.expertClue: Legislated false worship day enforced by civil power.
words.scripture: And he causeth all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and bond, to receive a mark in their right hand, or in their foreheads.
words.summary: A legislative decree that forces individuals to choose between obeying human laws or the immutable, holy law of God.

words.clue: The sign of allegiance to the beast power, received when one knowingly rejects God's Sabbath for a human substitute.
words.expertClue: Revelation 13:17 — required for buying and selling.
words.scripture: And that no man might buy or sell, save he that had the mark, or the name of the beast, or the number of his name.
words.summary: Receiving the mark represents submission to earthly powers over God. It stands in direct, active opposition to the Seal of God.

words.word: SEAL THE LAW AMONG MY DISCIPLES
words.clue: Isaiah links sealing God’s law among those who wait for Him.
words.expertClue: Isaiah 8:16 — Bind up the testimony, seal the law among my disciples
words.scripture: Bind up the testimony, seal the law among my disciples. 17 And I will wait upon the Lord, that hideth his face from the house of Jacob, and I will look for him. 18 Behold, I and the children whom the Lord hath given me are for signs and for w
words.summary: God’s seal involves loyalty to His law written in heart and life.

words.clue: The solemn moment when Christ finishes His intercessory sanctuary ministry and the destiny of all is fixed.
words.expertClue: Revelation 22:11 — character fixed; no mediator remains.
words.scripture: He that is unjust, let him be unjust still: and he which is filthy, let him be filthy still: and he that is righteous, let him be righteous still: and he that is holy, let him be holy still.
words.summary: When the intercession of Jesus ends, the door of mercy is closed forever. The righteous remain holy, and the wicked remain wicked.

words.clue: A period of unprecedented global crisis and distress that occurs after probation closes.
words.expertClue: Daniel 12:1 — trouble such as never was since there was a nation.
words.scripture: And at that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people: and there shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation...
words.summary: Without an intercessor, the earth experiences the full weight of human rebellion and plagues, but God preserves His faithful ones.

words.clue: The intense spiritual wrestling and anguish experienced by the saints as they plead for deliverance.
words.expertClue: Jeremiah 30:7 — Jacob's anguish, but saved out of it.
words.scripture: Alas! for that day is great, so that none is like it: it is even the time of Jacob's trouble, but he shall be saved out of it.
words.summary: Believers will review their lives, deeply repenting of any sin, and holding on to God by faith alone, refusing to let Him go.

words.clue: The final earthly decree dictating that those who refuse to worship the image of the beast should be killed.
words.expertClue: Revelation 13:15 — image speaks; refusers face execution.
words.scripture: And he had power to give life unto the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak, and cause that as many as would not worship the image of the beast should be killed.
words.summary: A decree will be passed to execute God's commandment-keeping people. But God will step in to deliver His saints.

words.clue: Daniel’s great prince stands for his people in unprecedented trouble.
words.expertClue: Daniel 12:1 — Michael stand up, the great prince
words.scripture: And at that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people: and there shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation even to that same time: and at that time thy people shall be delivered, every
words.summary: Deliverance is personal and divine when human mediation is finished.

words.clue: The glorious, literal, and visible return of Jesus Christ in the clouds of heaven.
words.expertClue: Titus 2:13 — the blessed hope and glorious appearing.
words.scripture: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ.
words.summary: The climax of the Christian hope. Jesus returns not in secret, but in supreme majesty, visible to every eye.

words.clue: The direction where a small cloud, about half the size of a man's hand, first appears to the waiting saints.
words.expertClue: Matthew 24:30 — sign of the Son of man seen in heaven's clouds.
words.scripture: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
words.summary: A small, dark cloud in the east is the sign of Christ's approach. As it draws near, it becomes a great, brilliant cloud of glory.

words.clue: The miraculous awakening of the dead in Christ at the sound of the trumpet.
words.expertClue: 1 Corinthians 15:42 — so also is the resurrection of the dead.
words.scripture: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption: It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
words.summary: Graves are opened worldwide, and the sleeping saints are raised with immortal bodies, reunited with their loved ones.

words.clue: The instantaneous change of the living righteous from mortality to immortality, caught up to meet the Lord.
words.expertClue: 1 Thessalonians 4:17 — living saints caught up to meet Him in the air.
words.scripture: Then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air: and so shall we ever be with the Lord.
words.summary: The living faithful are transformed without seeing death, joining the resurrected saints to meet Jesus in the air.

words.clue: John’s description of the public, visible return of Christ.
words.expertClue: Revelation 1:7 — every eye shall see him
words.scripture: Behold, he cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see him, and they also which pierced him: and all kindreds of the earth shall wail because of him. Even so, Amen. 8 I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the ending, saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to
words.summary: The Advent is not secret; the whole world will know when the King returns.

words.clue: The end-time group of believers who keep the commandments of God and have the testimony of Jesus.
words.expertClue: Revelation 12:17 — keep commandments and hold testimony of Jesus.
words.scripture: And the dragon was wroth with the woman, and went to make war with the remnant of her seed, which keep the commandments of God, and have the testimony of Jesus Christ.
words.summary: The final fraction of God's church, characterized by loyalty to His law and possession of the prophetic gift.

words.clue: God's restored, Edenic world recreated from the ashes of sin for the eternal home of the redeemed.
words.expertClue: Revelation 21:1 — first heaven and earth passed away.
words.scripture: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away; and there was no more sea.
words.summary: Sin is permanently erased. The earth is restored to its original beauty, and God dwells personally with His people.

words.clue: The majestic holy city that descends from God out of heaven to become the capital of the New Earth.
words.expertClue: Revelation 21:2 — holy city prepared as a bride adorned.
words.scripture: And I John saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down from God out of heaven, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
words.summary: The capital city of the saved, filled with the glory of God, built of gold and precious stones, where Christ and His saints reside.

words.clue: The glorious state of eternity where pain, sorrow, crying, and physical expiry are completely banished.
words.expertClue: Revelation 21:4 — God wipes every tear; pain and death are gone.
words.scripture: And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
words.summary: The ultimate triumph of Christ's redemption. Every trace of the curse of sin is wiped away, and eternal joy begins.

words.clue: God’s promise after tears and death are gone from the new creation.
words.expertClue: Revelation 21:4 — for the former things are passed away
words.scripture: the former things are passed away. 5 And he that sat upon the throne said, Behold, I make all things new. And he said unto me, Write: for these words are true and faithful. 6 And he said unto me, It is done. I am Alpha and Omega, the begi
words.summary: Sin’s history ends; the redeemed inherit a world where pain is only a memory.

words.clue: The heavenly tribunal where records are examined before Christ's return to earth.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:9-10 — books opened; the Ancient of days presides.
words.scripture: I beheld till the thrones were cast down, and the Ancient of days did sit... thousand thousands ministered unto him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him: the judgment was set, and the books were opened.
words.summary: Before Christ returns as King, heaven conducts a review of every life. This judgment vindicates God's character and confirms who is ready for eternity.

words.clue: Divine review that must finish before the Second Coming, not after it.
words.expertClue: 2300-day prophecy of Daniel 8:14 points to this cleansing era.
words.scripture: And he said unto me, Unto two thousand and three hundred days; then shall the sanctuary be cleansed.
words.summary: The Bible places judgment before the Advent. God's people are judged while probation still lingers, so the living may be prepared to meet Christ.

words.clue: The act of removing confessed transgressions from heavenly records during judgment.
words.expertClue: Acts 3:19 links repentance with sins being blotted out.
words.scripture: Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the Lord.
words.summary: Through faith in Christ, forgiven sins are erased from the books of heaven. This is the gospel assurance that motivates holy living.

words.clue: The antitypical Day of Atonement when the heavenly temple is purified from sin's record.
words.expertClue: Hebrews 8:1-2 — Christ ministers in the true tabernacle pitched by the Lord.
words.scripture: It was therefore necessary that the patterns of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
words.summary: Just as the earthly sanctuary was cleansed on the Day of Atonement, Christ now removes sin's defilement from the heavenly sanctuary through His blood.

words.clue: Daniel’s court scene before the Ancient of days.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:10 — the judgment was set, and the books were opened
words.scripture: judgment was set, and the books were opened. 11 I beheld then because of the voice of the great words which the horn spake: I beheld even till the beast was slain, and his body destroyed, and given to the burning flame. 12 As concerning t
words.summary: Heaven’s records undergird a fair judgment before the sentence of the ages.

words.clue: God permits believing a lie when truth is rejected — a final test of loyalty.
words.expertClue: 2 Thessalonians 2:11 — sent because they received not the love of the truth.
words.scripture: And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie.
words.summary: When humanity refuses to love truth, God withdraws restraining grace. The result is widespread deception that only Bible students will discern.

words.clue: Satan's end-time masquerade through spirits and signs imitating the dead and divine.
words.expertClue: Revelation 16:13-14 — three unclean spirits like frogs from dragon, beast, false prophet.
words.scripture: For they are the spirits of devils, working miracles, which go forth unto the kings of the earth and of the whole world, to gather them to the battle of that great day of God Almighty.
words.summary: Spiritualism will unite the world through miraculous displays. It is one of Satan's most successful tools to replace faith in Scripture with experience and emotion.

words.clue: Demonic agents released to perform signs and unite the world for the final conflict.
words.expertClue: Revelation 16:13 — frog-like spirits from the satanic trinity.
words.scripture: And I saw three unclean spirits like frogs come out of the mouth of the dragon, and out of the mouth of the beast, and out of the mouth of the false prophet.
words.summary: These spirits perform wonders that deceive rulers and peoples alike, preparing the world to oppose God and His faithful remnant.

words.clue: The symbolic gathering place for the final worldwide showdown between truth and rebellion.
words.expertClue: Revelation 16:16 — kings gathered to the place called in Hebrew Armageddon.
words.scripture: And he gathered them together into a place called in the Hebrew tongue Armageddon.
words.summary: Armageddon is not a literal valley battle alone, but the climactic conflict of the ages where all humanity chooses sides for or against God.

words.clue: Paul warns of Satan’s power with deceptive signs after the working of the lawless one.
words.expertClue: 2 Thessalonians 2:9 — all power and signs and lying wonders
words.scripture: power and signs and lying wonders, 10 And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth, that they might be saved. 11 And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion
words.summary: Miracles alone never prove truth; the Word is the test of every wonder.

words.clue: The enormous statue of mixed metals that filled the king's dream of successive world empires.
words.expertClue: Daniel 2:31 — a terrible, excellent image stood before the king.
words.scripture: Thou, O king, sawest, and behold a great image. This great image, whose brightness was excellent, stood before thee; and the form thereof was terrible.
words.summary: God revealed the outline of history through a single dream-image, showing that earthly kingdoms rise and fall under His sovereignty.

words.clue: The top of the image representing Babylon, the first kingdom in the prophetic succession.
words.expertClue: Daniel 2:38 — 'Thou art this head of gold.'
words.scripture: And wheresoever the children of men dwell, the beasts of the field and the fowls of the heaven hath he given into thine hand, and hath made thee ruler over them all. Thou art this head of gold.
words.summary: Babylon's glory is symbolized by gold — yet even the head of gold is temporary before the everlasting kingdom of God.

words.word: HIS FEET PART OF IRON AND PART OF CLAY
words.clue: The divided toes of the image showing a mixed, unstable end-time political order.
words.expertClue: Daniel 2:41 — feet and toes of iron mixed with miry clay.
words.scripture: And whereas thou sawest the feet and toes, part of potters' clay, and part of iron, the kingdom shall be divided; but there shall be in it of the strength of the iron, forasmuch as thou sawest the iron mixed with miry clay.
words.summary: The final phase of earthly power is strong yet brittle — never fully united until Christ returns.

words.word: STONE WAS CUT OUT WITHOUT HANDS
words.clue: The supernatural stone that smashes the image and becomes a mountain filling the whole earth.
words.expertClue: Daniel 2:34 — stone cut out without hands smote the image.
words.scripture: Thou sawest till that a stone was cut out without hands, which smote the image upon his feet that were of iron and clay, and brake them to pieces.
words.summary: Christ's kingdom is not of human origin. It ends all earthly empires and fills the earth with righteousness forever.

words.word: GOD OF HEAVEN SET UP A KINGDOM
words.clue: Daniel interprets the stone kingdom that fills the whole earth.
words.expertClue: Daniel 2:44 — the God of heaven set up a kingdom
words.scripture: And in the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed: and the kingdom shall not be left to other people, but it shall break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms, and it shall stand for ever. 45 Forasmuch as thou sawest that
words.summary: Human empires fall; Christ’s kingdom alone is everlasting.

words.clue: Four diverse animals that came up from the sea, representing four kings or kingdoms.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:3 — four great beasts came up from the sea.
words.scripture: And four great beasts came up from the sea, diverse one from another.
words.summary: From the turbulent sea of nations rise successive empires — a second line of prophecy paralleling the metallic image.

words.word: THE FIRST WAS LIKE A LION
words.clue: The first beast of Daniel 7, swift and royal, matching Babylon's golden head.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:4 — first beast like a lion with eagle's wings.
words.scripture: The first was like a lion, and had eagle's wings: I beheld till the wings thereof were plucked, and it was lifted up from the earth, and made stand upon the feet as a man, and a man's heart was given to it.
words.summary: Wings of speed give way to a humbled, humanized form — Babylon's pride is checked by the God of heaven.

words.word: RAISED UP ITSELF ON ONE SIDE
words.clue: Daniel's second beast — lopsided power devouring much flesh.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:5 — bear raised on one side with three ribs.
words.scripture: And behold another beast, a second, like to a bear, and it raised up itself on one side, and it had three ribs in the mouth of it between the teeth of it: and they said thus unto it, Arise, devour much flesh.
words.summary: Medo-Persia's lopsided strength and conquests fulfill the second stage of the four-beast vision.

words.clue: Third kingdom beast in Daniel 7 — swift empire with multiple heads.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:6 — leopard with four wings and four heads.
words.scripture: After this I beheld, and lo another, like a leopard, which had upon the back of it four wings of a fowl; the beast had also four heads; and dominion was given to it.
words.summary: Swift as a leopard, Greece under Alexander spreads quickly, then divides into four successor domains.

words.clue: Angel’s plain interpretation of the four great beasts from the sea.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:17 — These great beasts, which are four, are four kings
words.scripture: These great beasts, which are four, are four kings, which shall arise out of the earth. 18 But the saints of the most High shall take the kingdom, and possess the kingdom for ever, even for ever and ever. 19 Then I would know the truth of the
words.summary: Prophecy decodes empire after empire until the saints receive the kingdom.

words.clue: A horn that arises among ten, uprooting three, with eyes like a man and a mouth speaking great things.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:8 — another little horn with eyes and a mouth.
words.scripture: I considered the horns, and, behold, there came up among them another little horn, before whom there were three of the first horns plucked up by the roots: and, behold, in this horn were eyes like the eyes of man, and a mouth speaking great things.
words.summary: A religio-political power emerges after imperial Rome, marked by intelligence, speech, and the displacement of rivals.

words.clue: The little horn's blasphemous claims against the Most High.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:25 — speaks great words against the most High.
words.scripture: And he shall speak great words against the most High, and shall wear out the saints of the most High, and think to change times and laws: and they shall be given into his hand until a time and times and the dividing of time.
words.summary: Blasphemy, persecution of the saints, and attempts to alter divine times and laws identify this power in history and prophecy.

words.word: TIME AND TIMES AND THE DIVIDING OF TIME
words.clue: The prophetic period during which the saints are given into the little horn's hand.
words.expertClue: Daniel 7:25 — time, times, and the dividing of time.
